skills.description: Create, iterate, and scale paid ad creative for Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, TikTok, and similar platforms. Use when generating headlines, descriptions, primary text, or large sets of ad variations for testing and performance optimization.
trigger_conditions: Use when generating or iterating paid ad copy at scale.
anti_triggers: Do not use when performance data should inform the next round of creative.

skills.description: Generate comprehensive, developer-friendly API documentation from code, including endpoints, parameters, examples, and best practices
trigger_conditions: When you need to document a new API
When your API lacks clear documentation
anti_triggers: When the API is already well-documented
When the documentation is up-to-date with the codebase

skills.description: Master API documentation with OpenAPI 3.1, AI-powered tools, and modern developer experience practices. Create interactive docs, generate SDKs, and build comprehensive developer portals.
trigger_conditions: Improving API documentation quality and discoverability
anti_triggers: You only need a quick internal note or informal summary
The task is pure backend implementation without docs
There is no API surface or spec to document

skills.description: This skill provides a comprehensive guide for integrating the Google Gemini API into various applications, covering setup, text generation, streaming, multimodal inputs, function calling, model selection, and best practices.
trigger_conditions: Setting up Gemini API for the first time in a Node.js or Python project
Adding streaming responses to improve perceived latency
anti_triggers: Using `gemini-pro` for simple tasks — Flash is cheaper and faster
Sending large base64 images inline for files > 20MB — use File API instead

skills.description: Implement efficient similarity search with vector databases. Use when building semantic search, implementing nearest neighbor queries, or optimizing retrieval performance.
anti_triggers: the task is unrelated to similarity search patterns
you need a different domain or tool outside this scope
